summary: Matched Peach forward smash by replacing the remaining ftPe_AttackS4 assembly and an inline-asm placeholder with C, moving the object file from ftPe_11C1.c to ftPe_AttackS4.c, and wiring the ft_8008C390 caller to ftPe_AttackS4_Enter. The PR also improved Fighter x2218 flag representation from x2218_flag.bits.bN to direct named bitfields x2218_b0..x2218_b7, and used that cleanup across existing fighter code to support the match.
what_changed: Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_AttackS4.c with C implementations for ftPe_AttackS4_Enter, ftPe_AttackS4_Anim, ftPe_AttackS4_IASA, ftPe_AttackS4_Phys, and ftPe_AttackS4_Coll.
Deleted the old ftPe_11C1.c and ftPe_11C1.h inline-asm placeholder that exposed ftPe_8011C1C0.
Removed ftPe_AttackS4_Anim, ftPe_AttackS4_IASA, ftPe_AttackS4_Phys, and ftPe_AttackS4_Coll from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPe_Init.s.
Changed ft_8008C390 in asm/melee/ft/ft_0877.s to branch to ftPe_AttackS4_Enter instead of ftPe_8011C1C0.
Updated obj_files.mk to build ftPe_AttackS4.c.o instead of ftPe_11C1.c.o.
Renamed Peach fighter var fp->fv.pe.x8 to fp->fv.pe.attacks4_motion_id and typed it as FtMotionId.
In ftPe_Init_OnDeath, initialized fp->fv.pe.attacks4_motion_id to -1 instead of assigning fp->fv.pe.x8.
Added a union in Fighter at fp+2218 exposing x2218 as a u8 and direct bitfields x2218_b0 through x2218_b7.
Replaced many existing x2218_flag.bits.bN uses with direct x2218_bN accesses across Fox, Game & Watch, Mario, Mewtwo, Ness, Peach, Yoshi, Zelda, fighter.c, and ftdrawcommon.c.
Gave the remaining assembly helper at 8011C2F4 a named global symbol, ftPe_SpecialS_8011C2F4, and updated SpecialS/SpecialAirS callback pointer setup to reference it.
smart_moves: The new ftPe_AttackS4_Enter expresses Peach’s random forward-smash weapon selection in readable C using min/max FtMotionId bounds and a do-while loop that avoids repeating the previous motion id.
Typing Peach’s stored forward-smash motion field as FtMotionId made the random motion-state logic self-documenting and less error-prone than the old x8 u32.
Adding direct x2218_bN bitfields simplified codegen-sensitive flag accesses and avoided repeated x2218_flag.bits.bN nesting across multiple fighters.
The PR kept tiny callbacks simple: Anim only checks ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining before ft_8008A2BC, Phys delegates to ft_80084FA8, and Coll delegates to ft_80084104.
Renaming the assembly-local .L_8011C2F4 helper to ftPe_SpecialS_8011C2F4 preserved the remaining asm while making function-pointer references explicit and searchable.
The object-list swap preserved build ordering around Peach files while replacing the old address-based source name with the action-specific file name.
decomp_lessons: When a fighter action has an address-based stub name, move it to an action-named source/header once its semantics are understood; ftPe_8011C1C0 became ftPe_AttackS4_Enter.
For motion-state selection code, naming the stored previous motion id can reveal and document loop intent, here avoiding consecutive reuse of the same Peach forward-smash weapon.
Struct/header cleanup can be a matching tactic: a better fp+2218 bitfield layout enabled direct C expressions for flags used by AttackS4 and related reflect/absorb logic.
If only part of an asm file is matched, remove the matched routines but retain and name remaining asm helpers so callback pointer references stay explicit.
Build-system changes are part of a match: updating obj_files.mk from the old stub object to the new matched source object is required for the C to replace asm.
Simple action callbacks often match best as thin wrappers around existing common fighter helpers rather than reimplementing behavior.
naming_conventions: Use fighter/action callback names of the form ftPe_AttackS4_Enter, ftPe_AttackS4_Anim, ftPe_AttackS4_IASA, ftPe_AttackS4_Phys, and ftPe_AttackS4_Coll.
Replace address-only Peach source names like ftPe_11C1 with semantic action names like ftPe_AttackS4.
For unknown-but-indexed flag bytes, expose direct bitfield names matching offset and bit number, such as x2218_b0, x2218_b3, x2218_b6, and x2218_b7.
For fighter vars whose purpose is known, rename from offset placeholders to semantic fields, e.g. fv.pe.x8 to fv.pe.attacks4_motion_id.
Remaining assembly helpers can use subsystem-qualified names with their address suffix, e.g. ftPe_SpecialS_8011C2F4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted the ftPe_AttackS4 callbacks at 8011C248, 8011C284, 8011C2B4, and 8011C2D4 from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPe_Init.s into C.

Replaced inline asm for 8011C1C0 with C ftPe_AttackS4_Enter while preserving behavior: clear x2218_b0, clear x2200_ftcmd_var0, choose a non-repeating random motion id in the AttackS4 weapon range, call Fighter_ChangeMotionState, then ftAnim_8006EBA4.
Used direct bitfield members at fp+2218 to match loads/stores such as the original lbz/rlwinm flag checks more naturally in C.
Kept Phys and Coll as direct calls to common helpers ft_80084FA8 and ft_80084104, mirroring the original one-call assembly routines.
Updated the assembly branch target in ft_8008C390 to the newly matched ftPe_AttackS4_Enter without changing the branch layout.
Renamed a local asm label used as a callback address so lis/addi references target ftPe_SpecialS_8011C2F4 instead of .L_8011C2F4.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR context; no explicit reviewer feedback is available.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further rename Fighter x2218_b0 through x2218_b7 to semantic flag names.
Search for remaining ftPeach files with address-based names like ftPe_11C1 that can be moved to action-specific names.
Compare Peach AttackS4 motion-state selection against other random move-selection implementations using HSD_Randi.
Look for remaining assembly in asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPe_Init.s after the AttackS4 removal.
